blurb:
Thrill is a genuinely fun night out. The space is big and dramatic, two levels with a full bar, and the all-you-can-eat Korean BBQ is the main event. You cook the meat right at your table, which makes it a great option for a group that wants something a little more interactive than a regular dinner. It's also a solid pick if you're celebrating something.

The handcrafted cocktails are worth ordering alongside the BBQ. It's the kind of place where you end up staying longer than you planned.

They only open at 5pm Monday through Thursday, but on Friday and Saturday they stay open until 2am, so it's a good late-night option on weekends.
address: 5958 Roswell Rd, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 5:00 PM – 12:00 AM, Tuesday: 5:00 PM – 12:00 AM, Wednesday: 5:00 PM – 12:00 AM, Thursday: 5:00 PM – 12:00 AM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 2:00 AM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM

blurb:
Regal Buffet is the go-to when you've got a big group with mixed tastes and nobody can agree on what to eat. The place is roomy, the selection is large, and they have sushi rolls alongside the Chinese dishes, so there's genuinely something for everyone.

For the price, you're getting a lot. It's a good call for a casual family outing or when you just want to try a little of everything without committing to one dish. Nearly 3,000 reviews at a 4.1 rating tells you the locals have made up their minds about this place.

They close at 9pm on Sundays, a little earlier than the rest of the week, so plan accordingly if you're thinking about a Sunday night run.
address: 251 N Stemmons Fwy, Lewisville, TX 75067, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM

blurb:
The Select is the kind of spot that makes Sandy Springs feel like a real dining destination. The Art Deco design and open kitchen give it a cool, upscale feel without being stuffy, and the conservatory space is genuinely beautiful. This is a great pick for a date night, a work dinner, or any occasion where you want the setting to do some of the heavy lifting.

The menu is modern European, so expect thoughtfully prepared dishes rather than big casual plates. Sunday brunch starting at 10am is worth knowing about if you're looking for a special weekend morning out. They're dinner only Monday through Saturday, opening at 4pm each day.

Fridays and Saturdays they're open until midnight, which makes this one of the better late-night options in the area if you want a real meal after 10pm.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Suite 200, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 4:00 – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 4:00 – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 4:00 – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 4:00 – 11:00 PM, Friday: 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM, Saturday: 4:00 PM – 12:00 AM, Sunday: 10:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
Rosa's is the kind of Tex-Mex spot you go to when you want a solid meal without spending much or waiting long. They make their tortillas in-house, which sets them apart from a typical fast-food Mexican place. The whole operation is quick and low-key, good for a weekday lunch or a casual family dinner.

The classic Mexican dishes are straightforward and well-made. Nothing is going to surprise you, but that's kind of the point. You know what you're getting and it's consistently good.

They open at 6:30am every day, which is great if you're ever looking for a breakfast burrito before work.
address: 705 N Denton Tap Rd, Coppell, TX 75019, USA
hours: Monday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 6:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 6:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 6:30 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
If you haven't been to Rreal Tacos yet, put it on the list. The place has a great energy, the staff is genuinely helpful, and nearly 9,000 reviews at 4.9 stars tells you everything you need to know. It works for a casual weeknight dinner, a late-night run with friends, or really any time you want solid Mexican food without a lot of fuss.

People rave about the Esquite to start, and the pitcher drinks are worth ordering for the table. The Pineapple Guava and Passion Fruit pitchers both come up in reviews as refreshing and well-balanced, not too sweet, not too boozy.

They're open until midnight most nights, which is a bonus if you're coming from somewhere else and need a late bite.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 11:00 PM

blurb:
BJ's is one of those places that works for just about any group situation: coworkers after work, a casual date night, or the whole family after a Saturday game. The menu is big enough that everyone finds something they want, and they brew their own beer on site, which is a nice touch.

The house brews are worth trying if you're a beer person. The pub fare is solid comfort food, nothing fancy, but reliable and filling. It's the kind of place where you can linger a couple hours without feeling rushed.

They're open until midnight most nights and 1am on weekends, so it's also a decent late-night option when most other places around here have already closed the kitchen.
address: 2609 S Stemmons Fwy, Lewisville, TX 75067, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 1:00 AM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 1:00 AM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 12:00 AM

restaurant_name: Wu Wei Din Chinese Cuisine - Lewisville
blurb:
If you haven't been to Wu Wei Din yet, put it on your list. It's in a strip center off Stemmons but the food is genuinely good, and people around here keep going back for a reason. The orange chicken has a reputation for being the best in the area, and that's saying something.

The pan fried potstickers are a solid starter, and the lo mein vegetable noodles are worth ordering if you want something on the lighter side. The chicken udon noodles show up in a lot of reviews too. Portions are generous and the price is fair for what you get.

Just note they close between lunch and dinner every day, so don't show up at 3:30pm expecting to get seated. Plan ahead and you'll be fine.
address: 2505 S Stemmons Fwy #3480, Lewisville, TX 75067, USA

blurb:
If you haven't tried Persian food yet, Yalda is a great place to start. It's in the Aria development in Sandy Springs and it has a warm, polished feel that makes it perfect for a girls' night out, a date, or a special dinner with people you want to impress. The atmosphere is inviting and the service gets consistently high marks.

Reviewers rave about the hummus, the wings, and the chicken shish kabobs served over rice with lentils and raisins. The cocktail selection is solid too, and tiramisu is apparently the right way to finish the meal. Portions are generous and the flavors are bold without being complicated.

They close between lunch and dinner most days (roughly 2:45pm to 4pm), so plan around that if you're going mid-afternoon.
address: 6500 Aria Blvd Suite 500, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Brooklyn Cafe has been a Sandy Springs staple for a while, and it holds up. The vibe is warm and comfortable, with a nice patio when the weather cooperates. It's the kind of place that works for a lunch with a friend, a work dinner, or a low-key date night. The broad wine list is a bonus if you want to linger a little.

The menu is American bistro fare done well. Nothing too fussy, but a step above your average neighborhood spot.

One thing to know: they are closed on Sundays. So don't plan a Sunday dinner here.
address: 220 Sandy Springs Cir, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:30 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Sunday: Closed

blurb:
Not a restaurant, but worth knowing about if you're looking for something to do with the family on a weekend. Cinemark Lewisville has stadium seating and self-service kiosks so you're not stuck in a long line at the box office. The XD screen is the one to aim for if you're seeing anything action-heavy.

Over 2,600 reviews and a 4.3 rating says people keep coming back. It's a solid option when the kids need an outing and you don't want to make a whole production of it.
address: 2401 S Stemmons Fwy, Lewisville, TX 75067, USA

blurb:
Canton Cooks is a good one to keep in your back pocket when you want Chinese food that goes beyond the usual takeout routine. The menu is eclectic, with things like lobster in black bean sauce and BBQ duck that you don't see at most spots around here. It has a casual feel, so no need to dress up or make a big event of it.

The hot pot is a draw if you want something a little more interactive and fun for a group. For a simpler weeknight dinner, the BBQ duck is worth trying. It's the kind of place where you can order a few things and share.

Hours run until 10pm daily, with a slightly later opening on weekends at noon.
address: 5984 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Saturday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM, Sunday: 12:00 – 10:00 PM

blurb:
Canoe is the kind of place you save for a special occasion, and it delivers every time. It sits right on the Chattahoochee River with a gorgeous garden setting, so the atmosphere alone is worth the trip. This is a great pick for a milestone birthday, an anniversary, or anytime you want a dinner that feels like an actual event.

The kitchen does New American farm-to-table cooking, and they take it seriously. Everything on the menu changes with the seasons, so what you get is always fresh and thoughtfully put together. Lunch is a nice option too if you want the experience without the full dinner price tag.

Note that Saturday is dinner only, and Sunday they do brunch from 10:30am to 2:30pm. Reservations are a good idea, especially on weekends.
address: 4199 Paces Ferry Rd SE, Atlanta, GA 30339, USA

restaurant_name: Mister O1 Extraordinary Pizza Sandy Springs
blurb:
With a 4.9 rating across over 5,000 reviews, Mister O1 is not messing around. This is authentic Italian thin-crust pizza, the kind with a blistered crust and simple, quality ingredients, not the thick American chain stuff. Good for a casual dinner with the family or a low-key date night when you want something that actually tastes like it came from a real pizzeria.

They source ingredients locally or straight from Italy, and you can taste the difference. The antipasti and salads are worth ordering alongside your pizza, not just as an afterthought. It's in the Blue Stone Road shopping area in Sandy Springs, which makes it easy to get to from most of the Perimeter.

They're open every day starting at 11:30am and stay open until 11pm on Fridays and Saturdays, so it works well for a later dinner too.
address: 6405 Blue Stone Rd Ste 240, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Tuesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Wednesday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Thursday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM, Friday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Saturday: 11:30 AM – 11:00 PM, Sunday: 11:30 AM – 9:30 PM

blurb:
Hearth Pizza Tavern is right next door to Canton Cooks on Roswell Road, and it's one of the better sit-down pizza spots in Sandy Springs. The inside has an exposed brick look and a patio out front, so it feels a bit more like a night out than a typical pizza run. Good for date night or a casual dinner with another couple.

The pizzas lean creative, so if you like something a little more inventive than a standard pepperoni pie, this is your place. They have a solid beer and wine selection to go with it, which helps.

Open seven days a week starting at 11am, with Sunday closing a little earlier at 9pm.
address: 5992 Roswell Rd NE, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM

blurb:
Rreal Tacos has a 4.9 rating with over 9,000 reviews, which tells you pretty much everything you need to know. The vibe is lively and fun, good for a group dinner, a night out with friends, or honestly just a solid Tuesday when you want something better than fast food. The service gets a lot of love in reviews too, which is always a nice bonus.

Start with the Esquite before anything else. For drinks, the Pineapple Guava pitcher and the Passion Fruit pitcher are both crowd favorites and apparently go down very easy. They're open late every night of the week, until midnight Sunday through Saturday, so this works great as a late dinner spot.

It's inside the Sandy Springs Place shopping center, and parking is generally easy.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
